description: Improve MSSQL provisioning timing so setup cost is visible before behavior changes are made.
acceptance-criteria: `MssqlProvisioningTimingRecorder` records named setup phases, including create-empty-database, apply-generated-ddl, create-snapshot, restore-snapshot, backup-baseline, restore-backup, reset-database, and drop-database where applicable.
Timing records include FixtureSignature, GeneratedDdlHash, Phase, LeaseStrategy, Shard, TestWorkerId when available, and the existing caller file/member/line fields.
`MSSQL_FIXTURE_TIMINGS_PATH` remains the controlling environment variable for timing output.
`eng/ci/summarize-test-timings.ps1` or a companion summarizer groups MSSQL setup timing by shard, fixture signature, caller file, and phase.
The timing summarizer handles the new phase-level MSSQL fixture timing file without breaking existing TRX timing summaries or older total-only fixture timing records.
`test-timings-mssql-shard-*` artifacts include the raw `mssql-fixture-setup-timings.csv` file and any phase summary output needed for PR-run review.

The DMS API MSSQL GitHub Actions job also sets `MSSQL_FIXTURE_TIMINGS_PATH`, uploads a timing artifact, and participates in timing summaries for its MSSQL baseline-cache path without requiring an architecture change in Phase 0.
Timing artifact upload steps use `if: always()` so failed or flaky PR runs still leave setup diagnostics when timing files exist.
SchemaTools MSSQL integration usage is checked; if it remains on its CLI provisioning path instead of the generated-DDL test harness, that explicit out-of-scope rationale is documented.
No provisioning, leasing, or test-isolation behavior is changed in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully and produces MSSQL setup timing artifacts with phase-level baseline data for later comparison.
No Phase 1 work begins until the Phase 0 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.
steps-to-verify: Run: `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration`
Run any focused unit or integration tests added for the timing recorder and timing summarizer.
Before local MSSQL shard runs, verify `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin` points to a running SQL Server instance; start the local MSSQL test container if needed.

Run one local Backend MSSQL shard with timing enabled, for example: `MSSQL_FIXTURE_TIMINGS_PATH=./TestResults/mssql-fixture-setup-timings.csv dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'Category=MssqlCiShard1'`

Run the DMS API MSSQL integration suite with timing enabled, for example: `MSSQL_FIXTURE_TIMINGS_PATH=./TestResults/mssql-api-fixture-setup-timings.csv dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'Category=ApiIntegration&Category=MssqlIntegration'`
Run the timing summarizer against the generated timing artifact and confirm phase-level grouping is present.

Run: `rg -in 'mssql|generatedddl|createprovisionedasync|ddl provision' src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.SchemaTools src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.SchemaTools.Tests.Integration` and document whether SchemaTools needs follow-up work.

Run: `git diff -- speedup.md tasks.json eng/ci/summarize-test-timings.ps1 src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration`
Commit and push the phase branch.
Run: `gh pr view || gh pr create --fill`
Run: `gh run list --branch "$(git branch --show-current)" --commit "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" --limit 10`
Open the successful PR run, download the backend and DMS API MSSQL timing artifacts, and record the baseline timing summary in a PR comment before starting Phase 1.

description: Add a backend MSSQL baseline cache and convert a small high-value pilot set of repeated generated-DDL fixtures to lease from cached baselines.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 0 GitHub Actions baseline and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.

`MssqlBackendBaselineCache` exists in the backend MSSQL integration test project and caches `MssqlGeneratedDdlBaselineDatabase` instances by a deterministic fixture signature that includes fixture identity and strict-mode/profile inputs.

The backend cache stores the generated-DDL hash for each fixture signature and rejects signature reuse when the generated DDL hash changes, instead of hiding collisions by making the hash the only distinguishing key.
Backend MSSQL assembly cleanup disposes all cached baselines through `MssqlBackendBaselineCache.DisposeAllAsync()`.

A small high-value pilot set of repeated authoritative and profile fixtures is converted from direct `CreateProvisionedAsync(...)` calls to cached baseline leasing; broad conversion remains Phase 4 work.

Converted test classes use class-level leases plus `ResetAsync()` only when reset preserves isolation, use per-test leases when required, and leave direct provisioning in place for harness/provisioning behavior tests.
Cache creation, reuse, and generated-DDL hash mismatch behavior are observable through timing artifacts, test assertions, logs, or summary output.
Timing artifacts show fewer repeated `apply-generated-ddl` phases for the converted backend MSSQL pilot fixtures.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully and shows repeated backend MSSQL generated-DDL provisioning is reduced for the converted fixtures.
No Phase 2 work begins until the Phase 1 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.
steps-to-verify: Use the Phase 0 timing artifact to identify a small high-value repeated backend MSSQL pilot set before converting tests.
Before local MSSQL shard runs, verify `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin` points to a running SQL Server instance; start the local MSSQL test container if needed.
Run: `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common`

Run each affected local Backend MSSQL shard, for example: `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'Category=MssqlCiShard1'`
Repeat the shard command for `MssqlCiShard2`, `MssqlCiShard3`, and `MssqlCiShard4` when converted tests span those shards.
Run or add focused cache tests that prove same-signature/different-DDL reuse fails before any cached baseline is leased.
Run: `rg -n 'CreateProvisionedAsync\(' src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration` and confirm remaining direct callers are intentional.
Compare local or CI timing artifacts against Phase 0 for apply-generated-ddl count, total setup seconds, and cache creation/reuse evidence.
Commit and push the phase branch.
Run: `gh pr view || gh pr create --fill`
Run: `gh run list --branch "$(git branch --show-current)" --commit "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" --limit 10`
Open the successful PR run and record the converted fixture timing comparison in a PR comment before starting Phase 2.

description: Implement SQL Server backup-restore baseline leasing in the common MSSQL integration test harness.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 1 GitHub Actions validation and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.

`MssqlGeneratedDdlBackupBaselineDatabase`, `MssqlGeneratedDdlBackupBaselineLease`, and supporting database file metadata helpers are added to `EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common`.
`MssqlGeneratedDdlTestDatabase` can attach to an existing database name and connection string without reapplying generated DDL.

Backup creation provisions one baseline database, applies CI-friendly database options, checkpoints it, creates a COPY_ONLY backup with CHECKSUM and COMPRESSION, and avoids runner-filesystem path assumptions.
The provisioned baseline database used to create the backup is disposed or dropped after backup creation unless a measured reuse case requires keeping it.
Lease acquisition restores the baseline backup into a unique database with unique data and log file paths and returns an attached `MssqlGeneratedDdlTestDatabase`.
Lease disposal clears relevant connection pools and drops the leased database.
Dynamic SQL for database names, logical file names, and file paths uses helper-based identifier quoting and literal escaping consistently.

The backup baseline implementation keeps one backup per fixture signature, stores the generated-DDL hash for validation, guards backup creation with a shared lazy task, and rejects signature reuse with different generated DDL.
`RESTORE FILELISTONLY` is used to discover logical file names before restore, and restore targets are based on SQL Server-visible paths rather than GitHub runner paths.
Backup restores are not globally serialized unless timing proves a need; if throttling is added, it is opt-in through `MSSQL_BACKUP_RESTORE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`.
The implementation does not enable `xp_cmdshell` or require unsafe SQL Server cleanup features.
CI cleanup relies on container removal, and any local backup-file cleanup behavior is documented without requiring unsafe SQL Server features.
Integration tests cover independent leases, data isolation, lease database drop, generated-DDL hash mismatch, and concurrent lease file-name uniqueness.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully, backup-restore harness tests pass, and timing artifacts can identify backup-baseline and restore-backup phases.
No Phase 3 work begins until the Phase 2 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.
steps-to-verify: Before local MSSQL test runs, verify `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin` points to a running SQL Server instance; start the local MSSQL test container if needed.

Run existing baseline harness tests: `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~MssqlGeneratedDdlBaselineDatabase'`

Commit and push the phase branch.

description: Add strategy selection so MSSQL generated-DDL leases can be A/B tested between snapshot-slot and backup-restore behavior.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 2 GitHub Actions validation and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.
`MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_LEASE_STRATEGY` supports `snapshot-slot` and `backup-restore` values.
The initial default strategy remains `snapshot-slot` so existing CI behavior is preserved until rollout is proven.
Invalid `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_LEASE_STRATEGY` values fail fast with a clear error that lists supported values.
Permanent GitHub Actions rollout to `backup-restore` remains out of scope for this phase and is left to Phase 7.
Strategy selection is centralized in a small facade or cache-level switch instead of scattered through test classes.
Backend MSSQL and DMS API MSSQL baseline cache paths can use the selected lease strategy without changing individual test logic.
An experimental branch or workflow run can select `backup-restore` using only environment configuration.
Timing artifacts identify the selected lease strategy for each relevant timing record.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.

A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully with default snapshot-slot behavior, and a separate backup-restore PR or branch run records restore-backup timing for A/B comparison without permanently changing rollout settings.
No Phase 4 work begins until the Phase 3 default-strategy run and backup-restore A/B timing review are complete.
steps-to-verify: Before local MSSQL test runs, verify `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin` points to a running SQL Server instance; start the local MSSQL test container if needed.

Run: `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration`

Run focused backend MSSQL tests with the default strategy: `dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~MssqlGeneratedDdlBaselineDatabase'`

Run focused backend MSSQL tests with backup-restore selected: `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_LEASE_STRATEGY=backup-restore dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~MssqlGeneratedDdlBaselineDatabase|FullyQualifiedName~MssqlGeneratedDdlBackupBaseline'`
Run one focused strategy-selection test with an invalid `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_LEASE_STRATEGY` value and confirm the error is clear.
Run a focused DMS API MSSQL integration test with backup-restore selected if the API cache is wired in this phase.
Confirm timing records include `LeaseStrategy` values for snapshot-slot and backup-restore runs.
Commit and push the phase branch.

Run: `gh run list --branch "$(git branch --show-current)" --commit "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" --limit 10`
Open the successful PR run and record the default-strategy result plus backup-restore A/B timing comparison in a PR comment before starting Phase 4.

description: Convert backend MSSQL test classes by measured setup cost so expensive direct provisioning callers use cached leases safely.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 3 GitHub Actions validation and backup-restore A/B timing review are complete before implementation starts.
Phase 0 timing artifacts are used to choose conversion batches, or repeated authoritative/profile generated-DDL callers are used when timing data is unavailable.
High-impact profile merge/routing tests are converted before lower-cost callers when their isolation requirements allow it.
Authoritative sample, DS 5.2, DS 5.2 TPDM, link/reference, and descriptor integration tests are converted according to measured setup cost.
Each converted class follows the per-class decision checklist for schema mutation, reset behavior, fixture parallelism, and database-name/file-path assumptions.
Class-level leases keep `ResetAsync()` for data isolation; per-test leases are used for schema-mutating or unclear isolation cases.
Remaining direct `CreateProvisionedAsync(...)` callers are documented or clearly limited to harness/provisioning behavior tests.
Shard category changes are avoided in this phase unless required for correctness; performance rebalancing remains Phase 7 work.

CI timing artifacts show fewer full `apply-generated-ddl` phases and lower total backend MSSQL setup time; restore counts are interpreted as `restore-snapshot` under the default strategy or `restore-backup` in backup-restore A/B runs.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.

A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully with the default strategy, and any restore-backup performance claim is backed by a separate backup-restore GitHub Actions validation run.
No Phase 5 work begins until the Phase 4 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.

description: Optimize the remaining direct MSSQL generated-DDL provisioning path for tests that still require full database creation.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 4 GitHub Actions validation and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.
`MssqlTestDatabaseHelper.CreateDatabase(...)` or a specialized generated-DDL creation path avoids repeated data/log autogrowth when measurements justify explicit file sizing.
Generated-DDL provisioning sets appropriate SQL Server database options such as SIMPLE recovery when safe for tests.
Generated-DDL batch splitting remains correct and does not concatenate batches that require separate SQL Server compilation units.
Per-batch or sufficiently granular timing identifies slow generated-DDL batches.
`SqlConnection.ClearPool(...)` calls are retained only where a measured failure mode requires them.
An optional `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_PROVISION_MAX_CONCURRENCY` guard is available if timing shows hosted runners slow down during parallel full provisioning.
Harness/provisioning behavior tests still pass on the direct provisioning path.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully and shows remaining direct provisioning is faster or has measured concurrency control without new MSSQL failures.
No Phase 6 work begins until the Phase 5 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.

description: Optimize MSSQL reset behavior so class-level leased databases remain cheap to reuse between tests.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 5 GitHub Actions validation and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.
Reset SQL for generated-DDL databases is precomputed after DDL provisioning when the table and sequence set is fixed.
`ResetAsync()` avoids repeated metadata discovery when a precomputed reset plan is available.
Precomputed reset plans are scoped by fixture/database shape so tables, sequences, schemas, and descriptor/baseline exclusions cannot leak across different generated-DDL fixtures.
A truncate fast path is implemented only where SQL Server foreign-key rules allow it, with delete fallback for remaining tables.
Reset behavior preserves baseline tables while removing generated resource rows.
Identity values and sequences restart as expected after reset.
Foreign keys and triggers are enabled after reset, and trusted constraint behavior is preserved unless a measured and tested change is explicitly justified.
`WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT ALL` cost is measured before changing trusted constraint behavior.
Focused reset tests cover row cleanup, baseline preservation, identity/sequence restart, and FK/trigger reenablement.
No changes from later MSSQL setup-speedup phases are included in this phase.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully and shows reset-database timing improves for class-level lease reuse without isolation regressions.
No Phase 7 work begins until the Phase 6 PR run and timing-artifact review are complete.
steps-to-verify: Before local MSSQL test runs, verify `ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin` points to a running SQL Server instance; start the local MSSQL test container if needed.
Run: `dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Integration.Common src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration`
Run focused reset tests added for `MssqlDatabaseResetSql` and `ResetAsync()` behavior.
Run class-level lease heavy Backend MSSQL tests converted in Phase 4.
Run at least one full Backend MSSQL shard with timing enabled and compare reset-database phase duration against the previous phase under the same lease strategy.
Inspect any truncate fast path with fixtures that include foreign keys and descriptor/baseline tables.
Commit and push the phase branch.

Run: `gh run list --branch "$(git branch --show-current)" --commit "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" --limit 10`

description: Update GitHub Actions to use the new MSSQL lease behavior in CI and rebalance shards from measured setup cost.
acceptance-criteria: The Phase 6 GitHub Actions validation and timing-artifact review are complete before implementation starts.
Backend MSSQL shard jobs in `.github/workflows/on-dms-pullrequest.yml` set `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_LEASE_STRATEGY` to `backup-restore` after prior phase validation supports rollout.
Backend MSSQL shard jobs preserve the existing shard-specific `MSSQL_FIXTURE_TIMINGS_PATH` timing artifact path.
The DMS API MSSQL job is either rolled out to `backup-restore` with timing artifact validation or explicitly left on `snapshot-slot` with a documented rationale.
SchemaTools MSSQL is either confirmed independent of the generated-DDL harness optimization or tracked as a separate follow-up; Phase 7 does not silently claim SchemaTools gains.
`MSSQL_BACKUP_RESTORE_MAX_CONCURRENCY` and `MSSQL_GENERATED_DDL_PROVISION_MAX_CONCURRENCY` are set only if prior measurements justify CI throttling.
MSSQL tmpfs sizing is reviewed and adjusted only if backup-restore storage requirements exceed current capacity.
Backend MSSQL shards are rebalanced using timing artifacts that include setup cost, not only test counts.
Shard filters still select every intended backend MSSQL test exactly once.
At least one PR run confirms the slowest backend MSSQL shard moved down after workflow and shard changes.
At least three PR runs are reviewed after rollout before claiming the final no-flakiness success criterion.
The phase changes are committed and pushed to GitHub.
A PR exists for the branch; create one if it does not already exist.
A real GitHub Actions PR run for the pushed commit completes successfully and shows backend MSSQL CI uses backup-restore timing output and the slowest shard runtime is reduced.

Commit and push the phase branch.

Run: `gh run list --branch "$(git branch --show-current)" --commit "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" --limit 10`
Wait for all Backend MSSQL shard jobs and the DMS API MSSQL job to complete in GitHub Actions for the pushed commit.

Download the successful PR run timing artifacts and compare backend shard setup time, backend total shard time, DMS API MSSQL setup time, restore-backup counts, and apply-generated-ddl counts against the previous phase.
Confirm all intended Backend MSSQL shard categories are present exactly once.
Review two additional PR runs after rollout for MSSQL failures or timing regressions.

Add a PR comment with the validation run URLs, timing comparison, final backend/API lease strategy settings, concurrency settings, tmpfs decision, SchemaTools decision, shard balance summary, and three-run flakiness review.
